nodes.label: AI as strategic planning tool for content and scheduling
nodes.label_negative: AI as end-stage copy generator with vague input
nodes.grounding_span: I mostly use AI for the planning process of my work, especially in creating outlines and expanding on ideas in an outline

nodes.label: AI as creative catalyst that sparks human ideation
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for human creative execution
nodes.grounding_span: I enjoy the creative process a lot, and usually once I read an idea given to me by AI, my mind starts running a mile a minute

nodes.label: Effortful prompting for high-quality AI output
nodes.label_negative: Vague prompting yielding generic AI output
nodes.grounding_span: you get out exactly what you put into AI. If you ask for 'newsletter copy for a new product launch', you're going to get basic copy

nodes.label: AI as productivity tool for measurable outcomes
nodes.label_negative: AI as hype without proven results
nodes.grounding_span: It proved that there really is something to all this AI 'hype', and that it can be an amazing tool for productivity

nodes.label: Continuous skill evolution as job security
nodes.label_negative: Stagnation and mediocrity in the face of AI
nodes.grounding_span: one should only fear AI if they're mediocre at what they do. As a designer, I've always put effort into learning new skills

nodes.label: Creative fulfillment and enjoyment
nodes.grounding_span: I enjoy the creative process a lot

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: AI let's me skip the not so fun stuff like creating a schedule

nodes.label: Professional growth and adaptability
nodes.grounding_span: the need to grow and evolve has always been important for job security

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as planning tool
nodes.grounding_span: Seeing the results from the content schedule come to fruition was really satisfying

nodes.label: Confidence in own creative superiority over AI
nodes.grounding_span: I didn't even think about asking the AI for more details or ideas on compositions because I'm already gone and getting the photo shoot ready

nodes.label: Critique of colleagues' shallow AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I think if these people better planned out the newsletter in the first place, they would get stronger outcomes from the models

nodes.label: Optimistic aspiration for AI as project management tool
nodes.grounding_span: I would like to see it somehow turn into a fully automated project management tool

nodes.label: Iterative validation — uses AI ideas as base for real-world research
nodes.grounding_span: A lot of ideas given I would use as a base to do deeper research into similar products and niches to better see what is actually working

nodes.label: Effort-driven prompting — invests upfront detail for quality output
nodes.grounding_span: if you put in more effort and include things like product details, target audience, company info, and even samples of previous newsletters
